Creating Teams

To create a team, your game must call the XOnlineTeamCreate function. When it calls XOnlineTeamCreate, your title should pass in the team creator's ID. In addition, XOnlineTeamCreate has a parameter called pFirstTeamMemberProperties that should contain a pointer to an XONLINE_TEAM_MEMBER_PROPERTIES structure. One of the structure members, dwPrivileges, holds the user privileges for the team creator. Typically, your game sets this structure member to give the team creator permission to perform all administrative tasks on the team. The team creator is considered the "owner" of the team unless he or she leaves the team. Note that the team owner can leave the team only if another player has been assigned administrator privileges.

The Xbox Live service enforces a limit of 100 players on a team. The team creator must specify the maximum number of team members (up to the limit of 100) at the time the team is created. This limit cannot be changed later.

Calling XOnlineTeamCreate begins the asynchronous task of team creation. After it starts the task, XOnlineTeamCreate passes a task handle back to your program through the phTask parameter. Periodically, your game must call the XOnlineTaskContinue function to perform work on the task. When XOnlineTaskContinue indicates that the task is complete, your game gets the results by invoking the XOnlineTeamCreateGetResults function. Finally, it calls the XOnlineTaskClose function to close the task.
